summary: rollupSpend propagates OrderSummary.Currency to SpendBucket.Currency instead of hardcoding "USD"; falls back to "USD" only when the parser couldn't extract one.
reason: parser.parseOrderCard already extracts the per-order currency (e.g. "GBP" on amazon.co.uk, "EUR" on amazon.de). Hardcoding "USD" in the rollup mislabeled every non-US user's spend buckets. Freezing on the first order's currency per bucket is safe because a single Amazon TLD only emits one currency. Added spend_test.go covering both the GBP/EUR propagation and the empty-currency USD fallback.
